sections.paragraph:
βροτός, ή, όν mortal (opp. ἀθάνατος, θεός) subst. ὁ β. mortal pers. (Hom. et al.; Job freq.; TestSol 5:7 P; Mel., Fgm. 8b, 44) 1 Cl 39:4 (Job 4:17).—DELG.

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

pers. pers. = person(s)

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 184
